description: This PPG should be read in conjunction with APS 220 Credit Risk Management.
Under Attachment C of APS 220, ADIs must apply a buffer over a loan's interest rate of at least 3.0 per cent, unless determined otherwise by APRA.
APRA expects an ADI to have processes to determine whether loans approved through overrides would be treated as standard or non-standard loans for capital purposes under APS 112.
An ADI is required to have regard to the requirements governing security valuation practices as detailed in Attachment A of APS 220.
Defaulted exposure is defined by reference to the non-performing exposure definition in APS 220.
Defaulted property exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E rather than Attachment A.
Undrawn committed amounts included in the LVR loan amount calculation are determined in accordance with Attachment C.
Property valuations must be appraised independently using prudently conservative criteria consistent with the requirements set out in APS 220.
Unsettled and failed transactions must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment D to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.
Defaulted exposures must be risk-weighted in accordance with Attachment E to APS 112 rather than Attachment B.
Where Attachment B requires risk weights based on external credit ratings, ADIs must also comply with the requirements in Attachment F to APS 112.
Residential and commercial property exposures in a covered bond cover pool must meet the standard loan requirements in paragraphs 3 to 7 of Attachment A for covered bonds issued in Australia.
Unrated or non-qualifying covered bonds must be treated as bank exposures per Table 7; qualifying criteria are set out in paragraphs 15 and 16 of Attachment B.
Exposures through a third party as detailed in paragraph 41 must not be included as corporate exposures.
After applying CCFs, ADIs must risk-weight off-balance sheet exposures according to the requirements in Attachments A, B and D to APS 112.

Attachment G cross-references Attachment B of APS 112 for determining risk weights applicable to third-party banks holding cash collateral and for identifying sovereigns and financial institutions qualifying as core market participants.
Attachment G references Attachment J of APS 112 for the criteria that cash-funded credit-linked notes must fulfil to be treated as cash collateralised transactions.

Attachment F references Attachment I of APS 112 for the criteria that must be satisfied before a guarantee from a rated entity can be recognised for risk-weighting purposes for other entities within the same corporate group.
Paragraph 33 requires that SFTs covered by an eligible bilateral netting agreement must meet the requirements detailed in Attachment H to this Prudential Standard.
Paragraphs 6 and 8 of Attachment H reference Attachment G for eligible collateral criteria in the banking book and for the simple or comprehensive approach to RWA calculation.
The currency mismatch haircut on guarantees must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.
Cash-funded credit linked notes satisfying minimum credit derivative requirements may be treated as cash-collateralised transactions under Attachment G to APS 112.
The currency mismatch haircut on credit derivatives must be adjusted for actual revaluation frequency in accordance with paragraph 32 of Attachment G to APS 112.
Paragraph 46 refers to Attachment A for detailed requirements on collateral valuation policies and processes.
Paragraph 108 refers to Attachment B for the prescribed provisioning approach that APRA may permit or require an ADI to implement.
Attachment B references APS 112 for the definition of eligible lenders mortgage insurer and for the eligible collateral provisions relevant to cash on deposit deductions in prescribed provisioning.
